topic_probe_events.client_reply: Blaise: Well, I suppose I have my moments, you know? I mean, there are things I can do well, like organizing stuff or planning events. But then, there are times when I just feel... I don't know, like I’m not really measuring up to what others expect. It’s like, I see people around me who seem to have it all together, and I can’t help but think maybe I’m falling short in some way. It’s frustrating, really. I try to keep up, but sometimes it just feels overwhelming. I guess that’s just how it goes, right?

- Loss of control (Safety)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Blaise: Yeah, I think you might be onto something there. It’s like, when I feel that pressure, it’s almost as if I’m constantly trying to navigate this tightrope. If I slip up, I worry about what people will think, and that just adds to the anxiety. I guess it’s this fear of being judged or not being seen as good enough that really gets to me. I mean, I want to make my family proud, especially with everything going on with the wedding and all. It just feels like there’s so much riding on it, you know? I try to keep it all together, but sometimes it feels like I’m just one misstep away from everything falling apart.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Social isolation (Social Life)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Blaise: Yeah, I think that makes sense. Sometimes it does feel like I’m on my own with all of this. I mean, I try to talk to my friends about it, but they don’t always get it, you know? It’s like they have their own lives and struggles, and I don’t want to burden them with my issues. So, I end up keeping a lot of it to myself. I guess that just adds to the feeling of being isolated. I want to reach out, but there’s this nagging thought that maybe I should just handle it myself. It’s like, if I can’t manage this, then what does that say about me? I don’t know, it’s just a lot to carry sometimes.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Withdrawal pattern (Coping)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Blaise: Yeah, I think you’re right about that. It’s like I feel this pressure to be self-sufficient, to show that I can handle things without needing help. I guess it ties back to that whole idea of wanting to be seen as strong or capable, especially with everything happening around the wedding. I mean, I don’t want to add to anyone’s stress, and I worry that if I start sharing my struggles, it’ll just make things more complicated. It’s frustrating because I know deep down that everyone has their own battles, but I still feel like I have to keep mine under wraps. It’s just this constant tug-of-war in my head, you know? I want to reach out, but then I get caught up in this cycle of overthinking and second-guessing myself.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Domestic stress (Housing/Home)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Blaise: Yeah, I think that makes sense. I mean, my home environment definitely plays a role in all of this. There’s this unspoken expectation that we should all be doing well and supporting each other, but it often feels like we’re just tiptoeing around each other’s issues instead of actually addressing them. It’s like there’s this pressure to maintain a facade of everything being fine, especially with the wedding coming up. I know my parents want things to be perfect, and I feel like I have to uphold that image. So, when I’m struggling, it’s hard to reconcile that with what’s expected of me. It’s like I’m stuck in this cycle of trying to keep up appearances while also feeling like I’m falling apart inside. It’s frustrating because I want to be honest about how I’m feeling, but I also don’t want to disrupt the fragile balance we have.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Financial hardship (Economy)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Blaise: Yeah, I think you're onto something there. The financial aspect definitely adds another layer of stress. I mean, weddings can be so expensive, and there’s this expectation that everything should be perfect, which just amplifies the pressure. I worry about how much my parents are spending and if they’re feeling the strain. It’s like I don’t want to add to their worries, but at the same time, I feel this weight of responsibility to make everything work out. It’s frustrating because I want to enjoy this time, but instead, I’m caught up in all these worries about money and expectations. It’s like I’m stuck between wanting to celebrate and feeling like I have to manage everyone’s feelings about the whole situation. It’s exhausting, really.

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Body image distress (Health)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Blaise: You know, that’s an interesting point. I hadn’t really thought about it in that way, but I guess there is some pressure there too. I mean, with all the attention on the wedding, it’s hard not to think about how I look and how I’m perceived. There’s this expectation to look my best, and I find myself worrying about whether I’ll measure up to what everyone else expects. It’s like I’m constantly comparing myself to others, and that just adds another layer of anxiety. I want to feel good about myself, especially on a day that’s supposed to be joyful, but instead, I’m caught up in these thoughts about how I should look or how I might be judged. It’s frustrating because I want to focus on the celebration, but these self-image concerns keep creeping in and overshadowing everything else. It’s just another thing to juggle, you know?

topic_probe_events.prompt_context:
- Loneliness exposure (Safety)

Explicitly discuss this topic in normal, client-friendly language. Ask whether it fits the client, while leaving room for them to say it does not.
topic_probe_events.client_reply: Blaise: Yeah, I think you’re onto something there. It does feel lonely at times, especially when I’m caught up in these comparisons and feeling like I’m not enough. It’s like I’m surrounded by people, but I still feel this disconnect, like I’m on the outside looking in. I see others who seem to have it all together, and I can’t help but feel like I’m missing something. It’s frustrating because I want to connect with my family and friends, but these feelings of inadequacy just push me further away. I guess it’s that fear of being judged or not being accepted for who I am that keeps me from reaching out. I want to be part of the celebration and feel that joy, but instead, I end up feeling isolated in my own thoughts. It’s a tough cycle to break, you know?
